Title: The mechanism for brassinosteroids suppressing climacteric fruit ripening

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ACC to ethylene in the final step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during pear fruit ripening; reduced by EBR and enhanced by Brz
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit during postharvest ripening; higher when PuBZR1 is silenced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PuACO1 expression/activity promotes ethylene production and fruit ripening; BR-activated PuBZR1 suppresses both PuACO1 activity and transcription, reducing ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; pull-down; BiFC; luciferase complementation imaging; co-IP; purified enzyme activity assay; fruit ACO activity assay; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-PCR; GUS reporter assay; RT-qPCR; transient PuBZR1 silencing with metabolite/enzyme profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key ethylene biosynthetic enzyme; terminal determinant step
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous epibrassinolide (EBR); brassinazole (Brz); room-temperature postharvest storage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression delays ripening and maintains firmness, which may delay desirable ripening traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PuBZR1 interacts with PuACO1 regions containing Fe2+ binding sites, suggesting inhibition of cofactor-dependent activity; BR enhances PuBZR1-PuACO1 interaction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PuACO1 is a central ethylene-forming enzyme promoting pear fruit ripening. 2. BR-activated PuBZR1 directly interacts with PuACO1 and inhibits its enzyme activity. 3. PuBZR1 also binds the PuACO1 promoter and represses its transcription, jointly lowering ethylene production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for delaying climacteric ripening and extending shelf life; could be combined with BZR1/ACS regulatory engineering, but excessive suppression may impair normal ripening quality

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts SAM to ACC, supplying the direct precursor for ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during ripening and significantly suppressed by EBR; increased when PuBZR1 is silenced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PuACS1a expression promotes ACC and ethylene biosynthesis; BR-activated PuBZR1 and repression of PuERF2 reduce PuACS1a transcription, lowering ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP-PCR; GUS reporter assay; PuERF2 ChIP-PCR and GUS activation assay; transient PuBZR1 silencing
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression can delay ripening and firmness loss, potentially delaying edible ripening
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PuBZR1 binds a BRRE motif in the promoter and represses it directly; PuERF2 binds and activates the promoter independently of EBR without PuBZR1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PuACS1a is the major BR-responsive ACS gene linked to ripening-associated ethylene biosynthesis in pear. 2. PuBZR1 directly represses the PuACS1a promoter. 3. PuERF2 activates PuACS1a, and PuBZR1 indirectly suppresses PuACS1a through repression of PuERF2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to modulate preclimacteric ethylene and shelf life; may be combined with ACO1/BZR1 manipulation for stronger control

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ACC to ethylene in the final step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed by EBR and induced by Brz in postharvest apple fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Commercial-harvest apple fruit during room-temperature storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced MdACO1 expression correlates with lower ethylene production under BR treatment in apple
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ethylene profiling after EBR/Brz treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as homologous validation in apple; direct mechanistic assays were not performed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdACO1 transcript abundance decreases after EBR treatment and increases after Brz treatment. 2. Its expression pattern parallels BR-mediated suppression or promotion of apple ethylene production. 3. The apple response supports conservation of the pear mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for extending apple storage life through BR-linked ethylene control

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts SAM to ACC, the direct precursor of ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed by EBR and induced by Brz in postharvest apple fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Commercial-harvest apple fruit during room-temperature storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced MdACS1 expression correlates with lower ethylene production under BR treatment in apple
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ethylene profiling after EBR/Brz treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression may delay desired fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Apple evidence is correlative/homology-based within this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdACS1 transcript abundance decreases after EBR treatment and increases after Brz treatment. 2. Its expression changes accompany BR-mediated changes in apple ethylene output. 3. This supports a conserved BR-ethylene regulatory module in climacteric fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate lever for BR-responsive control of apple shelf life and climacteric onset

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BZR family brassinosteroid signaling transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm and nucleus; BR enhances nuclear accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Gradually decreases during fruit development; induced by EBR and repressed by Brz; suppressed by ethylene during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Postharvest ripening fruit and fruit calli; silenced fruit show reduced transcript abundance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of PuACO1, PuACS1a, and PuERF2; direct protein-mediated inhibition of PuACO1 enzyme activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses PuACO1 and PuACS1a transcription directly; represses PuERF2, thereby indirectly reducing PuACO1 and PuACS1a expression; inhibits PuACO1 activity by protein interaction
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Direct binary interaction with PuACO1 supported; no interaction with PuERF2 detected
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Brassinosteroids/EBR activate PuBZR1 accumulation and nuclear translocation; Brz reduces activity; ethylene suppresses PuBZR1 expression during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely part of a negative interplay with ethylene; ethylene inhibits PuBZR1 expression, suggesting feedback weakening of BR suppression during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the central BR-activated suppressor of ethylene biosynthesis and climacteric ripening in pear
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major BR-responsive suppressor linking BR signaling to reduced ethylene output through both transcriptional and post-translational control
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; Y2H screen; pull-down; BiFC; luciferase complementation; co-IP; subcellular localization; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-PCR; GUS assays; transient antisense silencing in fruit; ethylene and ACO activity measurements
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: EBR; Brz; room-temperature postharvest storage; ethephon/1-MCP mentioned for expression context
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher PuBZR1 delays ripening and maintains firmness, potentially postponing desirable eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BR-enhanced interaction with PuACO1 does not depend on tested unphosphorylation mimic S102A/S106A; PuBZR1 does not interact with PuERF2 and works upstream of PuERF2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PuBZR1 is the only strongly BR-responsive BZR homolog in ripening pear fruit. 2. It suppresses ethylene biosynthesis by directly repressing PuACO1 and PuACS1a transcription and by repressing the activator PuERF2. 3. It also directly interacts with PuACO1 to inhibit ACO activity, making PuBZR1 the central BR-dependent anti-ripening regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for extending shelf life and slowing climacteric ripening via BR signaling engineering; best deployed with careful tuning to avoid over-delayed ripening
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in transient expression/calli assays

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed by EBR during fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Postharvest pear fruit and fruit calli
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PuACO1 and PuACS1a promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Repressed by BR-activated PuBZR1; not directly responsive to EBR without PuBZR1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: No evidence that PuERF2 regulates PuBZR1; no feedback to PuBZR1 promoter detected
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene biosynthetic gene expression; its repression by PuBZR1 contributes to BR-mediated reduction of ethylene production
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important intermediate activator through which PuBZR1 indirectly suppresses ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-PCR; GUS activation assay
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation may accelerate ripening and soften fruit
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PuERF2 binds DRE motifs in PuACO1 and PuACS1a promoters; its binding/activity was not EBR-dependent in absence of PuBZR1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PuERF2 is the only tested ERF strongly suppressed by BR treatment in pear fruit. 2. It directly binds and activates PuACO1 and PuACS1a promoters. 3. PuBZR1 represses PuERF2 transcription, creating an indirect route for BR-mediated suppression of ethylene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to fine-tune ripening speed downstream of BR signaling; suppression may extend shelf life
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in transient expression/calli assays

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BZR family brassinosteroid signaling transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increased by EBR and decreased by Brz in postharvest apple fruit
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdACO1; MdACS1 (inferred from homologous response, not directly tested)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely transcriptional repression in BR response; direct mechanism not dissected in apple in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: MdBZR1 increase accompanies MdACO1 and MdACS1 decrease under EBR treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with BR-mediated suppression of apple ethylene production and ripening
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports conservation of BR-BZR1 anti-ripening control across climacteric fruits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ethylene profiling under EBR/Brz treatment
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced expression may delay normal ripening
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Apple evidence is supportive and correlative; direct promoter binding/protein interaction assays were not performed
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBZR1 transcript abundance rises with EBR and falls with Brz treatment in apple. 2. These changes are inversely associated with MdACO1/MdACS1 expression and ethylene production. 3. The data support a conserved BR-BZR1 mechanism in apple fruit ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for delaying apple ripening and improving storage performance through BR pathway manipulation
